title: Result
description: Terminal receipt — at most one per accepted request (FR7). Callers branch on status, terminal_phase, and reason_code, never on human-readable messages (FR8). Semantic combination laws (which status/phase/reason triples are legal) are Go admission validation's job.

request_sha256.description: Digest of the exact accepted request bytes (D5) — no canonical re-encode.

work_sha256.description: Digest of the exact submitted work.json bytes (D5); identical across placements of the same work.

workload_exit_code.description: Absent when no workload process ran. Zero is not sufficient for success (D7).

placement.description: Non-authoritative placement receipt (D6): what backend profile and immutable inputs actually ran. Consumers may display these details but cannot infer generic state from them.

image_sha256.description: Absent for placements with no image identity.

stream_delivery.description: Delivery mode for declared workload event artifacts, not stdout/stderr tailing. 'live' is reserved and NOT valid in v0 (D11).

enforced.description: Resolved profile constraints actually enforced; shape is backend-specific.

details.description: Opaque backend detail; never a source of generic state.

causes.description: Ordered prior {phase, reason_code} values when a later failure became primary, such as cleanup failure after deadline expiry.

diagnostics.description: Structured records that may name an uncertain allocation without changing primary status.
